title: sour springy venezuelan aubergine tart
description: a tangy and refreshing venezuelan-inspired tart featuring baby aubergine, coconut cream, and a crunchy coconut crust. perfect for springtime or anytime you want a taste of venezuela at home.

ingredients.name: baby aubergine
ingredients.notes: any color, but purple is most traditional.

ingredients.name: coconut cream
ingredients.notes: full-fat for the best flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: coconut flour
ingredients.notes: make sure to sift before using.

ingredients.name: granulated sweetener
ingredients.notes: such as yacon or monk fruit, optional.

ingredients.name: zest of 2 limes
ingredients.notes: use a microplane for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: coconut oil
ingredients.notes: melted for the filling, solid for the crust.

instructions: sift the coconut flour and tapioca flour into a large bowl, then stir in the granulated sweetener, salt, and water until a sticky dough forms.
press the dough into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch (23cm) tart pan with a removable bottom.
bake the crust for 15 minutes, then let it cool completely.
in a blender or food processor, combine the coconut cream, eggs, lime zest, vanilla extract, and melted coconut oil. blend until smooth and creamy.
pour the coconut cream mixture into the baked tart crust, then smooth the top with a spatula.
bake the tart for an additional 25-30 minutes, or until the filling is set and the edges are lightly golden brown.
remove the tart from the oven and let it cool completely on a wire rack.
once the tart has cooled, sprinkle the unsweetened shredded coconut over the top for garnish.
serve chilled, preferably within 24 hours of baking.
keep leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
reheat leftovers in the oven or microwave, but be aware that the texture may change slightly.
